symptoms: White or gray powdery spots on leaves, stems, or flowers.
treatment: Remove infected parts. Improve air circulation. Apply neem oil or sulfur-based fungicide.

symptoms: Yellowing leaves, stunted growth, mushy black or brown roots.
treatment: Reduce watering. Ensure proper drainage. Repot in fresh, well-draining soil. Trim affected roots.

symptoms: Tiny webbing on plants, yellow speckling on leaves.
treatment: Wipe leaves with a damp cloth. Increase humidity. Use insecticidal soap or neem oil.

symptoms: Small green, black, or brown insects clustered on new growth. Sticky honeydew on leaves.
treatment: Spray off with a strong stream of water. Use insecticidal soap or neem oil. Introduce ladybugs.

symptoms: Brown, black, or yellow spots on leaves, sometimes with halos.
treatment: Avoid overhead watering. Remove heavily infected leaves. Provide good air circulation. Use a copper-based fungicide if severe.

symptoms: Grayish-brown fuzzy mold on leaves, stems, or flowers. Often occurs in cool, damp conditions.
treatment: Improve air circulation. Reduce humidity. Remove infected plant debris. Avoid overhead watering.

symptoms: Orange, rust-colored, or brown pustules on the undersides of leaves.
treatment: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Water at the base of the plant. Apply a sulfur-based or copper fungicide.

symptoms: Small, hard, bump-like insects on stems or leaves. Yellowing leaves and sticky honeydew.
treatment: Prune heavily infested parts. Dab pests with rubbing alcohol. Use horticultural oil or neem oil.

symptoms: White, cottony masses on stems, leaf joints, or undersides of leaves.
treatment: Wipe off with rubbing alcohol using a cotton swab. Wash plant with insecticidal soap. Apply neem oil.

symptoms: Tiny black flies near the soil surface. Seedlings or young plants may wilt.
treatment: Allow the top inch of soil to dry between waterings. Use yellow sticky traps. Water with a diluted hydrogen peroxide solution or Bti (Bacillus thuringiensis israelensis).
